title: Code Generation with Claude Code
description: Configure Claude Code for a large TypeScript monorepo with frontend (React), backend (Node.js/Express), and infrastructure (Terraform) directories. Different teams have different coding standards. The setup must enforce ESLint rules for frontend, strict error handling for backend, and Terraform naming conventions for infra — all automatically, not as suggestions.
architecture_notes: Use the 3-level CLAUDE.md hierarchy: global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md for universal preferences, project CLAUDE.md for shared conventions, and .claude/rules/ scoped files with path globs for per-directory standards. Use PostToolUse hooks to automatically run the relevant linter after each file write (ESLint for .tsx files, custom validation for .tf files). Configure MCP servers in .claude/settings.json for database access during development. Define slash commands for common workflows: /review, /test, /commit.
key_patterns: scoped rules with path: glob frontmatter
skills for schema/context, tools for actions
officialDescription: You are using Claude Code to accelerate software development. Your team uses it for code generation, refactoring, debugging, and documentation. You need to integrate it into your development workflow with custom slash commands, CLAUDE.md configurations, and understand when to use plan mode vs direct execution.
